qcm_integration.overview: This file provides detailed instructions for integrating QSE findings with Quantum Cryptographic Manager (QCM) using the gcm_api MCP tool.

qcm_mcp_server.description: The gcm-mcp-server provides tools to interact with IBM Guardium Cryptographic Manager (GCM/QCM) APIs. It enables uploading QSE findings to QCM for centralized cryptographic asset management and policy enforcement.
qcm_mcp_server.authentication: Authentication is handled automatically if GCM_USERNAME and GCM_PASSWORD environment variables are set. Otherwise, authentication may be required before API operations.

when_to_upload.trigger: When findings JSON file is generated

prerequisites.prerequisite: Findings JSON file must exist in explorer results directory
QSE import profile must exist in QCM

operation.name: list_import_profiles
operation.purpose: Retrieve list of import profiles to find QSE profile ID

response_handling.action: Parse the response to find profiles
Look for QSE-related profile (check name or description)
Store profile ID for use in upload operation

error_scenarios.scenario: No profiles found - inform user to create QSE profile first
Multiple QSE profiles - ask user which one to use
API error - report error and check connectivity

operation.name: upload_findings
operation.purpose: Upload QSE findings JSON file to QCM

parameter.name: profile_identifier
parameter.description: The profile ID obtained from import_profiles.list

parameter.name: file_path
parameter.description: Absolute path to the findings JSON file
parameter.note: MUST be an absolute path. Construct by combining working directory with qse_explorer_result/filename

validation_before_upload.check: Verify file exists at specified path
Verify file is valid JSON format

success.action: Inform user that findings are now in QCM
Provide any upload ID or reference from response

failure.action: Check if file path is correct
Check if profile ID is valid
Inform user findings remain available locally

file_location_detection.description: QSE generates findings files in the explorer results directory within the current working directory where the scan was executed. The exact filename may vary based on scan type and timestamp.

file_patterns.pattern: *.json (any JSON file in explorer results directory)

detection_strategy.step: Sort by modification time (most recent first)
Select the most recent findings file
Verify file is not empty and contains valid JSON
Construct full path relative to working directory

file_location_detection.important: The explorer results directory is created in the working directory where the QSE scan command was executed, NOT in the QSE installation directory. Always use paths relative to the current working directory.

practice.title: Always verify file exists before upload
practice.description: Use list_files to confirm the findings file exists in explorer results directory and get its exact absolute path before attempting upload to avoid errors.

practice.title: Always use absolute paths for upload
practice.description: The gcm_api tool requires absolute paths for file uploads. Always construct the full absolute path by combining the working directory with qse_explorer_result/filename (e.g., /Users/user/project/qse_explorer_result/findings.json).

practice.title: Cache profile ID when possible
practice.description: If running multiple scans in sequence, store the profile ID from the first lookup to avoid repeated API calls.

practice.title: Provide clear error messages
practice.description: When upload fails, provide specific guidance on what went wrong and how to fix it. Always mention that findings remain available locally.

practice.title: Handle authentication gracefully
practice.description: If authentication fails, inform user about required environment variables (GCM_USERNAME, GCM_PASSWORD) or authentication requirements.

practice.title: Validate JSON before upload
practice.description: Optionally read and validate the JSON file structure before uploading to catch issues early.

practice.title: Update repository URL before upload
practice.description: Always update the repositoryUrl field in the findings JSON before uploading to QCM. This ensures proper tracking and correlation of findings with the source repository. Use sed command to update without reading the entire file, and validate JSON structure after modification.

practice.title: Create backup before modifying JSON
practice.description: When updating the repositoryUrl field, use sed with -i.bak flag to create a backup file. This allows recovery if the JSON structure is corrupted during the update process.

scenario.name: profile_not_found
scenario.symptoms: import_profiles.list returns empty or no QSE profile

resolution.step: Inform user that QSE import profile must be created in QCM
Provide guidance on creating import profile in QCM UI
Do not proceed with upload
Remind user that findings are available locally in ./qse_explorer_result/

scenario.name: file_not_found
scenario.symptoms: Findings file doesn't exist at expected location

resolution.step: List contents of explorer results directory
Check if directory exists in working directory
Check if scan actually completed successfully
Look for alternative file names in the directory
If no file found, inform user scan may have failed
Report the specific error message from API
Verify file path is correct and accessible
Verify profile ID is valid
Check QCM server connectivity
Suggest retrying the upload
Remind user findings are still available locally
Check if GCM_USERNAME and GCM_PASSWORD are set
Inform user about authentication requirements
Suggest setting environment variables
Do not proceed with upload until authenticated
Verify QSE scan actually ran and completed
Check if scan was run from a different directory
List current working directory contents
Inform user that scan may not have completed successfully

scenario.name: github_url_not_available
scenario.symptoms: git remote command fails or returns non-GitHub URL

resolution.step: Inform user that GitHub repository URL is needed
Ask user to provide the GitHub repository URL
Validate that provided URL is a valid GitHub repository URL (contains github.com)
Do not proceed with repositoryUrl update until valid URL is obtained

scenario.name: repository_url_update_fails
scenario.symptoms: sed command fails or JSON validation fails after update

resolution.step: Check if findings file is writable
Verify backup file (.bak) was created
If JSON is corrupted, restore from backup: mv [file].bak [file]
Report specific error to user
Suggest manual update of repositoryUrl field if automated update continues to fail
Do not proceed with QCM upload if repositoryUrl is not updated

complete_workflow_example.description: Complete example of QCM integration workflow after QSE scan

step.title: Locate findings file in working directory
step.note: Directory is relative to current working directory

step.title: Update Repository URL in findings JSON

actions.action: Get GitHub URL from git remote origin
If git command fails or returns non-GitHub URL, ask user for URL
Update repositoryUrl field using sed command
Validate JSON structure and extract repositoryUrl value

step.note: Creates backup file with .bak extension before modification
step.important: Must be completed before uploading to QCM

step.title: Upload findings to QCM
step.note: Use absolute path - findings file now contains updated repositoryUrl

step.title: Confirm completion
step.action: Mention local file location for reference

user_communication.success_message: QSE scan completed successfully. Findings have been uploaded to Quantum Cryptographic Manager (QCM) and are now available for analysis and policy enforcement. The findings file remains available locally at [absolute_path_to_file].
user_communication.partial_success_message: QSE scan completed successfully. However, upload to QCM failed: [error_details]. The findings file is available locally at [absolute_path_to_file] and can be manually uploaded or the upload can be retried.
user_communication.failure_message: QSE scan completed but findings file could not be located. Please check the explorer results directory in your working directory for scan results.
